blocks.content: Saturday mornings at Hester's. The same booth they'd sat in since middle school, coffee for her, sweet tea for him, and the whole morning spread out ahead of them with nowhere to be.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: What do you want to make first. When you go back.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Something real. Not an assignment. Something I actually have to say.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You already know what it is.
His mom's back porch on a Sunday. Daisy reading. Him watching her read and not bothering to pretend otherwise.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: And you're just going to let me.
The whole group at the diner one evening, no occasion, just everyone in the same place because that's what summer in Millhaven was.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: When you two get married I want a credit.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: In the program. Eli Turner — ambient lighting and emotional infrastructure.
August came. The same week it always did.
